title: argemone glauca
intro: Argemone glauca, also known as Mexican prickly poppy, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily for its alkaloids, particularly sanguinarine and dihydro-sanguinarine, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Consuming Argemone glauca is said to provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Argemone glauca is often used in the form of capsules, tinctures, or infusions, and is typically taken in small doses to avoid potential toxicity.
